character_description: The youngest Stark child, Rickon is known for his stubbornness and loyalty to his family. He is captured and killed by Ramsay Bolton during the Battle of the Bastards.
A member of the Brave Companions, Rorge is a brutal and violent criminal encountered by Arya Stark.
The elderly servant at Winterfell, famed for her eerie stories and tales about White Walkers and historical legends.
A renowned smith in King's Landing, known for forging quality armor and weapons.
An Ironborn soldier who serves Theon Greyjoy during his occupation of Winterfell.
Winterfell’s kennelmaster, loyal to the Stark family.
A young knight promoted quickly after Jon Arryn’s death, killed in the Hand’s tournament by Gregor Clegane.
Leader of the sellsword company, the Stormcrows, later executed by Daario Naharis.
Daughter of Cersei and Jaime Lannister, sent to Dorne as a ward, poisoned as part of Ellaria Sand's revenge plot.
Meereenese noble who marries Daenerys Targaryen to broker peace in Meereen.
One of Walder Frey’s sons, involved in the Red Wedding.
A ruthless Frey family member, prominent during the Red Wedding.
The mercenary leader also known as 'The Titan's Bastard.'
A member of the Brave Companions, notorious for torturing prisoners, especially at Harrenhal.
A minor Braavosi character in Arya Stark's storyline.
A wildling leader recognized by his bone armor, fights in the wars beyond the Wall.
An Ironborn reaver who serves Theon Greyjoy in his campaigns.
A young cousin in the Lannister family, captured and killed as a prisoner of the Starks.
Another Lannister cousin, held as a hostage and murdered in retaliation by Rickard Karstark.
A mysterious sorceress from Asshai who offers cryptic guidance to Daenerys Targaryen.
A wildling clan chieftain from the Vale, known for his ferocity in battle.
Chief of the Burned Men, a notorious clan among the mountain tribes of the Vale.
Bloodrider sworn to Daenerys Targaryen.
Another loyal Dothraki bloodrider in service to Daenerys.
A financial envoy from the Iron Bank of Braavos who negotiates with Stannis Baratheon and others in Westeros.
Younger brother to Tywin Lannister, skilled administrator and Hand of the King under Tommen Baratheon.
Supporter of House Arryn in the Vale’s politics.

character_titles: Knight in Service to the Lannisters
character_description: A brutal knight involved in the violence surrounding the War of the Five Kings.
A prostitute in King's Landing who has a child by King Robert Baratheon.
Head of the Alchemists’ Guild, helps produce wildfire for Tyrion’s defense of King's Landing.
A spy and lover in King's Landing, associated with Loras Tyrell and the brothels.
A sadistic servant and lover to Ramsay Bolton.
Champion chosen for trial by combat at the Eyrie, defeated by Bronn.
Jailer of House Arryn in the Eyrie.
Dothraki handmaiden serving Daenerys Targaryen.
Minstrel who becomes involved in the intrigue at the Eyrie and falsely accused by Petyr Baelish.
Has mysterious visions and helps Bran Stark with his powers and the journey north.
Orphan boy traveling with Arya Stark; killed by Rorge.
Bloodrider to Khal Drogo, killed after opposing Daenerys.
Wealthy merchant who helps arrange the marriage of Daenerys to Khal Drogo.
Member of the Brotherhood Without Banners.
Devout son of Davos Seaworth, serving Stannis Baratheon in battle.
Disgraced former maester serving as Cersei Lannister's Master of Whisperers; creator of Ser Gregor's monstrous form.

character_titles: First Ranger of the Night's Watch
character_description: Ned Stark's brother, a heroic Night’s Watch ranger who disappears beyond the Wall and later aids Bran Stark.
Trusted advisor to Stannis Baratheon, dies attempting to poison Melisandre.
Handmaiden who teaches Daenerys Targaryen about pleasure and betrayal.
A sadistic knight in the Kingsguard, executed by Arya Stark.
Wife to Stannis Baratheon with a fanatic devotion to the Lord of Light.
Trusted Ironborn raider supporting Theon Greyjoy.
Notorious for sacrificing his sons to White Walkers in exchange for peace.
A magical and sinister warlock, opposes Daenerys Targaryen.
Loyal Stark retainer, killed by Jaime Lannister.
Resourceful companion who helps protect Bran Stark on his journey.
A cruel member of the Night’s Watch; involved in harming Samwell Tarly and others.
Unlucky and drunken knight favored by Sansa Stark; killed by Petyr Baelish after Joffrey’s death.
Wildling with the ability to skinchange; distrusts Jon Snow.
Lannister cousin imprisoned with Jaime Lannister, killed during escape.
Gentle youngest son of Cersei, naively manipulated as King before dying by suicide after Cersei's destruction of the Sept of Baelor.
Young boy from the Gift; joins the Night’s Watch and famously kills Jon Snow in a mutiny.
Head of House Tyrell and father to Margaery and Loras; dies when the Sept of Baelor is destroyed.
Tutor to the Stark daughters in Winterfell; killed during the sack of King's Landing.
Wealthy merchant of Qarth who refuses Daenerys Targaryen's request for ships.
Healer and blood magician whose actions lead to Khal Drogo's death and Daenerys's loss.
Opportunistic commander of King's Landing's City Watch; executed by Jon Snow for cowardice and betrayal.
Cersei's cousin and lover; joins the Faith Militant, killed in the Sept explosion.
Father of Theon and Yara; leads the failed Greyjoy rebellion, killed by his brother Euron.
Leader of the Thenn wildlings, fights in the attack on the Wall.
Infamous for his brutal training of the Unsullied; outsmarted by Daenerys.
Sickly and spoiled ruler of the Vale, manipulated by Petyr Baelish.
Vengeful Stark bannerman; executed by Robb Stark for killing Lannister prisoners.
Legendary ranger who sacrifices himself so Jon Snow can infiltrate the wildlings.
Instigator of political plots; murdered by Petyr Baelish.
Harsh trainer at Castle Black, opposes Jon Snow's leadership.
Loyal squire to Tyrion Lannister and later Brienne of Tarth.
Wildling who protects Bran and Rickon Stark; killed by Ramsay Bolton.
Sharp-witted prostitute who becomes a spy in King's Landing.
Jon Snow’s friend at Castle Black, killed fighting the wildlings.
Comic relief companion to Arya Stark; finds his calling as a baker.
Unacknowledged bastard son of Robert Baratheon; becomes Storm's End's lord after Daenerys's rise.
Sansa’s uncle; captured during the Red Wedding, later released to become the Lord of Riverrun again.

character_titles: Acting Lord Commander of the Night's Watch
character_description: One of Jon Snow’s closest friends and a loyal defender of the Wall.
Staunch Stark ally and determined matriarch.
Son of Gilly, adopted by Samwell Tarly, amongst the Night's Watch at the Wall.
Nameless assassin who trains Arya Stark in the ways of the Faceless Men.
Cunning and charming mercenary who serves and romances Daenerys.
A cruel Bolton mercenary who captures Jaime Lannister.
Infamous orchestrator of the Red Wedding.
Veteran warrior and uncle of Catelyn Stark; dies defending Riverrun.
Brave member of Jon Snow's Night's Watch cohort, dies bravely defending the Wall.

character_titles: Former Lord Commander of the Kingsguard
character_description: Legendary knight, dismissed by Joffrey and later serving Daenerys.
Freed from slavery, becomes Daenerys’s closest confidante and translator.
Member of the ancient Children of the Forest, helps Bran Stark beyond the Wall.
Kind mentor to the Stark children in Winterfell, perishes in the sack of Winterfell.
Pious wife of Stannis Baratheon, devout follower of the Lord of Light.
Fierce pirate daughter of Balon, commands fleets and pledges loyalty to Daenerys.
Gentle giant whose true name is Wylis, sacrifices himself to save Bran Stark.
Leader of the Brotherhood Without Banners, frequently resurrected by Thoros of Myr.
Married into House Bolton for alliance, murdered by Ramsay after the birth of her son.
One of Daenerys’s three dragons, named after her brother Rhaegar; killed by Euron Greyjoy.
Stark bannerman, killed in the Battle of the Bastards fighting for Ramsay Bolton.
Fought for the Lannisters, executed by Daenerys for refusing to bend the knee.
Esteemed nobleman of the Vale, supports Sansa Stark and Jon Snow.
Shrewd matriarch of House Tyrell; masterminded Joffrey’s murder, died defying Cersei.
Performer in Braavos who helps Arya Stark recover after an attempt on her life.
Scholar who teaches Samwell Tarly at the Citadel.
Spymaster who serves the realm above all, helping bring about Jon Snow's ascendance.
Charismatic pirate king, kills his brother and seeks power through alliances and terror.
Stern father of Samwell, commands Lannister forces, executed by Daenerys.
Enforcer who trains and battles Arya with the Faceless Men.
Crafty and compassionate queen through marriages to Renly, Joffrey, and Tommen; killed by Cersei’s plot.
Sworn sword driven by her own sense of honor, vows herself to serve and protect Sansa Stark.
Relentless claimant to the throne, killed in battle outside Winterfell.
Exiled knight, faithful servant, and protector of Daenerys.
Tyrion’s lover who betrays him at court; killed by Tyrion in revenge.
Cold and cunning bannerman to the Starks, betrays Robb Stark at the Red Wedding.
Stark ward turned outcast, redeems himself by saving Sansa and dying for Bran’s defense.
Craster’s daughter and wife, rescued by Samwell Tarly.

character_titles: Three-Eyed Raven, King of the Six Kingdoms
character_description: Young Stark crippled in childhood, attains supernatural sight and is crowned king at the series end.

character_titles: Grand Maester, Lord of Horn Hill
character_description: Savior of the realm through intellect and bravery, advances to become Grand Maester for Bran Stark.
Former smuggler, loyal advisor to Stannis and Jon Snow.
Sorceress and adviser to Stannis and Jon; resurrects Jon Snow and helps defeat the Night King.
Wildling warrior and close friend to Jon Snow; fights proudly in the Great War.
Wildling archer and Jon Snow’s lover, dies in the Battle at Castle Black.
Disciplined and loyal general freed by Daenerys; leads her troops until the series finale.
Devious manipulator whose plots eventually catch up to him when Sansa and Arya execute him.
Survivor who grows from naïve princess to decisive ruler, crowned Queen of the North.
Notorious and fearsome fighter who seeks redemption and battles his brother, dying in the collapse of King's Landing.
Renowned swordsman, infamous for killing Aerys II (the Mad King). Struggles with guilt and dies with Cersei.
Cruel and petulant boy king, poisoned at his own wedding in one of the series’ most memorable moments.

character_titles: Queen of the Andals and the First Men
character_description: Ruthless and cunning matriarch who dies under the Red Keep’s collapse.
Young Stark assassin who kills the Night King and sails west of Westeros.

character_titles: Lord Commander of the Night's Watch, King in the North
character_description: Unacknowledged son of Eddard Stark, revealed as a Targaryen, kills Daenerys to save Westeros.

character_titles: Hand of the King, Hand of the Queen
character_description: Witty, intelligent and often underestimated, plays a pivotal role throughout the series.

character_titles: Mother of Dragons, Queen of Meereen
character_description: Exiled princess who conquers and frees the oppressed, tragically killed by Jon Snow after burning King's Landing.
